```{r}
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Cluster = sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster.)
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Axis1 = `pcoa_coords_nocontaminants$Axis.1`)
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Axis2 = `pcoa_coords_nocontaminants$Axis.2`)
  
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$Cluster <- as.factor(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$Cluster)

wilc_results <- list()
bacteria_plots <- list()
pdf('KWPlots_Nocontaminants.pdf')

# Perform the tests and generate plots
for(i in 4:ncol(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance)) {
    print(i)

    # Extract data for all clusters
    data_cluster1 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster1[, i])
    data_cluster2 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster2[, i])

    # Combine data for Kruskal-Wallis test
    all_data <- c(data_cluster1, data_cluster2)
    group_labels <- c(rep("Cluster1", length(data_cluster1)), 
                      rep("Cluster2", length(data_cluster2)))

    # Skip if any group has no variation or insufficient data
    if (length(unique(all_data[!is.na(all_data)])) <= 1) {
        warning(paste("Skipping column", i, "- insufficient data or no variation."))
        next
    }

    # Perform Kruskal-Wallis test
    kruskal_result <- tryCatch(kruskal.test(all_data ~ group_labels), 
                               error = function(e) NA)

    # Check if Kruskal-Wallis test returned valid results
    if (is.na(kruskal_result$p.value)) {
        warning(paste("Skipping column", i, "- Kruskal-Wallis test failed."))
        next
    }

    wilc_results[[paste0("kruskal_", i)]] <- kruskal_result$p.value

    # Post-hoc pairwise Wilcoxon test if Kruskal-Wallis is significant
    if (kruskal_result$p.value < 0.05) {
        pairwise_results <- pairwise.wilcox.test(all_data, group_labels, 
                                                 p.adjust.method = "BH", exact = FALSE)
        wilc_results[[paste0("pairwise_", i)]] <- pairwise_results$p.value
        
        # Calculate medians for plotting
        clus1_median <- median(data_cluster1, na.rm = TRUE)
        clus2_median <- median(data_cluster2, na.rm = TRUE)

        # Generate boxplot with statistical annotations only if significant
        bacteria_plots[[i]] <- ggplot(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance, aes(y = sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance[, i], x = Cluster)) +
            geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
            geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0) +
            theme_minimal() +
            scale_y_sqrt() +
            labs(y = paste0(colnames(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance)[i])) +
            stat_compare_means(method = "kruskal.test", label.y = max(all_data, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1)

        print(bacteria_plots[[i]])
    }
}

dev.off()

```

### 24.1.5 Combined Dataframe with Abundance data and cluster data (Work with HILA samples from here)
```{r}
combined_data_nocontaminants.rel <- merge(hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_nocontaminants, 
                                      salter_nocontaminants.rel, by = "row.names")

rownames(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel) <- combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$Row.names
combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$Row.names <- NULL

```

### 24.1.6 Encode Progression as a factor
```{r}
combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$progressed <- as.factor(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$progressed)
```

### 24.1.7 Create a Survival Object

```{r}
survival_obj_abbs_nocontaminants.rel <- Surv(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free,
                                         combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$progressed)
```

### 24.1.8 Run Boruta on the Survival Object

```{r}
boruta_nocontaminant_survfit.rel <- Boruta(salter_nocontaminants.rel, survival_obj_abbs_nocontaminants.rel, pValue = 0.05, maxRuns = 10000, doTrace = 2)

boruta_nocontaminants_finaldecision.rel <- as.data.frame(boruta_nocontaminant_survfit.rel$finalDecision)

write.csv(file = "Boruta_RelAb_Nocontaminants_FinalDecision.csv", boruta_nocontaminants_finaldecision.rel)
```

## 24.2 Consensus Dataframe
### 24.2.1 Finding the ideal number of clusters for the cancer samples ----
Using the dataframes generated in 6.1.1
``` {r}

class(salter_consensus.rel)
salter_consensus.rel_matrix <- as.matrix(salter_consensus.rel)
dist_matrix_genus_consensus <- dist(salter_consensus.rel_matrix, method = "manhattan")
pcoa_genus_consensus <- pcoa(dist_matrix_genus_consensus, correction = "none", rn = NULL)
biplot_genus_consensus <- biplot.pcoa(pcoa_genus_consensus, plot.axes = c(1,2), dir.axis1=1, dir.axis2=1, rn = NULL, main = "PCOA Plot for No Contaminant Dataframe")
biplot_genus_consensus

fviz_nbclust(salter_consensus.rel, kmeans, method='silhouette') 
fviz_nbclust(salter_consensus.rel, kmeans, method='gap_stat') 
fviz_nbclust(salter_consensus.rel, kmeans, method='wss') 

#There is no real separation with the no contaminants dataframe so I might just go with 2
# Upon visualising I realised 3 might be a better number

#Optimal Number of Clusters - 3 
```

### 24.2.2 PCOA plot with clusters and co-ordinates

```{r}
set.seed(198)
gap_stat <- clusGap(salter_consensus.rel, FUN = kmeans, nstart = 25,
                    K.max = 10, B = 50)
print(gap_stat, method = "firstmax")

pcoa_genus_consensus$vectors
pcoa_coords_consensus <- as.data.frame(pcoa_genus_consensus$vectors)
head(pcoa_coords_consensus)
pcoa_coords_final1 <- as.data.frame(pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.1)
pcoa_coords_final2 <- as.data.frame(pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.2)
pcoa_coords_genus_final <- cbind(pcoa_coords_final1, pcoa_coords_final2)

kmeans_genus <- kmeans(pcoa_coords_genus_final, 3, nstart = 20)
kmeans_genus
kmeans_plot <- plot(pcoa_coords_genus_final, col = (kmeans_genus$cluster + 1), main = "K-Means Clustering of Samples - Consensus Dataframe", xlab = "", ylab = "", pch = 20, cex = 2)
kmeans_plot

kmeans_clusterdata <- as.data.frame(kmeans_genus$cluster)
sample_list <- cbind(kmeans_clusterdata, pcoa_coords_genus_final)
head(sample_list)
rownames(salter_consensus.rel)
rownames(sample_list) <- rownames(salter_consensus.rel)
head(sample_list)
sample_cluster_list <- cbind(sample_list, hila_samples_metadata_nodeath)
head(sample_cluster_list)
write.csv(file = "HILA_Sample_Cluster_List_Consensus.csv", sample_cluster_list)
ggplot(sample_cluster_list, aes(x=pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.1, y=pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.2, color = progressed)) + geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) + geom_text(aes(label = rownames(sample_list)))

```

### 24.2.3 Separate samples into their individual clusters and with relative abundances

```{r}
genus_abundance_clusters <- cbind(sample_list$`kmeans_genus$cluster`, salter_consensus.rel)
write.csv(file = "Genus_Abundance_Cluster.csv", genus_abundance_clusters)
genus_abundance_clusters <- read.csv("Genus_Abundance_Cluster.csv", row.names = 1)

sample_cluster_coords <- as.data.frame(sample_cluster_list)[, 2:3] 
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- cbind(sample_cluster_coords, genus_abundance_clusters) 

genus_cluster1 <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>% filter(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster. == 1)
genus_cluster2 <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>% filter(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster. == 2)
genus_cluster3 <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>% filter(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster. == 3)
```

### 24.2.4 Create a scatterplot for Cluster 1, 2 and 3 with labels

```{r}
ggplot(genus_cluster1, aes(x = genus_cluster1$`pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.1`, y = genus_cluster1$`pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.2`, label = rownames(genus_cluster1))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(genus_cluster1)), vjust = -0.5, size =3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster with UTI samples (Cluster 1)")

ggplot(genus_cluster2, aes(x = genus_cluster2$`pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.1`, y = genus_cluster2$`pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.2`, label = rownames(genus_cluster2))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(genus_cluster2)), vjust = -0.5, size =3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster with UTI samples (Cluster 2)")

ggplot(genus_cluster3, aes(x = genus_cluster3$`pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.1`, y = genus_cluster3$`pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.2`, label = rownames(genus_cluster3))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(genus_cluster3)), vjust = -0.5, size =3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster with UTI samples (Cluster 3)")

```

### 24.2.5 Boxplots for the three clusters

```{r}
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Cluster = sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster.)
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Axis1 = `pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.1`)
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Axis2 = `pcoa_coords_consensus$Axis.2`)
  
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$Cluster <- as.factor(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$Cluster)

wilc_results <- list()
bacteria_plots <- list()
pdf('KWPlots_Consensus.pdf')

# Perform the tests and generate plots
for(i in 4:ncol(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance)) {
    print(i)

    # Extract data for all clusters
    data_cluster1 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster1[, i])
    data_cluster2 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster2[, i])
    data_cluster3 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster3[, i])

    # Combine data for Kruskal-Wallis test
    all_data <- c(data_cluster1, data_cluster2, data_cluster3)
    group_labels <- c(rep("Cluster1", length(data_cluster1)), 
                      rep("Cluster2", length(data_cluster2)), 
                      rep("Cluster3", length(data_cluster3)))

    # Skip if any group has no variation or insufficient data
    if (length(unique(all_data[!is.na(all_data)])) <= 1) {
        warning(paste("Skipping column", i, "- insufficient data or no variation."))
        next
    }

    # Perform Kruskal-Wallis test
    kruskal_result <- tryCatch(kruskal.test(all_data ~ group_labels), 
                               error = function(e) NA)

    # Check if Kruskal-Wallis test returned valid results
    if (is.na(kruskal_result$p.value)) {
        warning(paste("Skipping column", i, "- Kruskal-Wallis test failed."))
        next
    }

    wilc_results[[paste0("kruskal_", i)]] <- kruskal_result$p.value

    # Post-hoc pairwise Wilcoxon test if Kruskal-Wallis is significant
    if (kruskal_result$p.value < 0.05) {
        pairwise_results <- pairwise.wilcox.test(all_data, group_labels, 
                                                 p.adjust.method = "BH", exact = FALSE)
        wilc_results[[paste0("pairwise_", i)]] <- pairwise_results$p.value
        
        # Calculate medians for plotting
        clus1_median <- median(data_cluster1, na.rm = TRUE)
        clus2_median <- median(data_cluster2, na.rm = TRUE)
        clus3_median <- median(data_cluster3, na.rm = TRUE)

        # Generate boxplot with statistical annotations only if significant
        bacteria_plots[[i]] <- ggplot(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance, aes(y = sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance[, i], x = Cluster)) +
            geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
            geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0) +
            theme_minimal() +
            scale_y_sqrt() +
            labs(y = paste0(colnames(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance)[i])) +
            stat_compare_means(method = "kruskal.test", label.y = max(all_data, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1)

        print(bacteria_plots[[i]])
    }
}

dev.off()

```

### 24.2.6 Combined Dataframe with Abundance data and cluster data
```{r}
combined_data_consensus.rel <- merge(hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_consensus, 
                                      salter_consensus.rel, by = "row.names")

rownames(combined_data_consensus.rel) <- combined_data_consensus.rel$Row.names
combined_data_consensus.rel$Row.names <- NULL

```

### 24.2.7 Encode Progression as a factor
```{r}
combined_data_consensus.rel$progressed <- as.factor(combined_data_consensus.rel$progressed)
```

### 24.2.8 Create a Survival Object

```{r}
survival_obj_abbs_consensus.rel <- Surv(combined_data_consensus.rel$overall_progression_free,
                                         combined_data_consensus.rel$progressed)
```

### 24.2.9 Run Boruta on the Survival Object

```{r}
boruta_consensus_survfit.rel <- Boruta(salter_consensus.rel, survival_obj_abbs_consensus.rel, pValue = 0.05, maxRuns = 10000, doTrace = 2)

boruta_consensus_finaldecision.rel <- as.data.frame(boruta_consensus_survfit.rel$finalDecision)

write.csv(file = "Boruta_RelAb_Consensus_FinalDecision.csv", boruta_consensus_finaldecision.rel)
```


## 24.3 All Contaminant Dataframe
### 24.3.1 Finding the ideal number of clusters for the cancer samples ----
Using the dataframes generated in 6.1.1
``` {r}
class(salter_allcontaminants.rel)
salter_allcontaminants.rel_matrix <- as.matrix(salter_allcontaminants.rel)
dist_matrix_genus_allcontaminants <- dist(salter_allcontaminants.rel_matrix, method = "manhattan")
pcoa_genus_allcontaminants <- pcoa(dist_matrix_genus_allcontaminants, correction = "none", rn = NULL)
biplot_genus_allcontaminants <- biplot.pcoa(pcoa_genus_allcontaminants, plot.axes = c(1,2), dir.axis1=1, dir.axis2=1, rn = NULL, main = "PCOA Plot for No Contaminant Dataframe")
biplot_genus_allcontaminants

fviz_nbclust(salter_allcontaminants.rel, kmeans, method='silhouette') 
fviz_nbclust(salter_allcontaminants.rel, kmeans, method='gap_stat') 
fviz_nbclust(salter_allcontaminants.rel, kmeans, method='wss') 

#There is no real separation with the no contaminants dataframe so I might just go with 2
# Upon visualising I realised 3 might be a better number

#Optimal Number of Clusters - 3 
```


### 24.3.2 PCOA plot with clusters and co-ordinates

```{r}
set.seed(198)
gap_stat <- clusGap(salter_allcontaminants.rel, FUN = kmeans, nstart = 25,
                    K.max = 10, B = 50)
print(gap_stat, method = "firstmax")

pcoa_genus_allcontaminants$vectors
pcoa_coords_allcontaminants <- as.data.frame(pcoa_genus_allcontaminants$vectors)
head(pcoa_coords_allcontaminants)
pcoa_coords_final1 <- as.data.frame(pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.1)
pcoa_coords_final2 <- as.data.frame(pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.2)
pcoa_coords_genus_final <- cbind(pcoa_coords_final1, pcoa_coords_final2)

kmeans_genus <- kmeans(pcoa_coords_genus_final, 3, nstart = 20)
kmeans_genus
kmeans_plot <- plot(pcoa_coords_genus_final, col = (kmeans_genus$cluster + 1), main = "K-Means Clustering of Samples - All Contaminants Dataframe", xlab = "", ylab = "", pch = 20, cex = 2)
kmeans_plot

kmeans_clusterdata <- as.data.frame(kmeans_genus$cluster)
sample_list <- cbind(kmeans_clusterdata, pcoa_coords_genus_final)
head(sample_list)
rownames(salter_allcontaminants.rel)
rownames(sample_list) <- rownames(salter_allcontaminants.rel)
head(sample_list)
sample_cluster_list <- cbind(sample_list, hila_samples_metadata_nodeath)
head(sample_cluster_list)
write.csv(file = "HILA_Sample_Cluster_List_AllContaminants.csv", sample_cluster_list)
ggplot(sample_cluster_list, aes(x=pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.1, y=pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.2, color = progressed)) + geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) + geom_text(aes(label = rownames(sample_list)))

```


### 24.3.3 Separate samples into their individual clusters and with relative abundances

```{r}
genus_abundance_clusters <- cbind(sample_list$`kmeans_genus$cluster`, salter_allcontaminants.rel)
write.csv(file = "Genus_Abundance_Cluster.csv", genus_abundance_clusters)
genus_abundance_clusters <- read.csv("Genus_Abundance_Cluster.csv", row.names = 1)

sample_cluster_coords <- as.data.frame(sample_cluster_list)[, 2:3] 
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- cbind(sample_cluster_coords, genus_abundance_clusters) 

genus_cluster1 <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>% filter(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster. == 1)
genus_cluster2 <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>% filter(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster. == 2)
genus_cluster3 <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>% filter(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster. == 3)
```

### 24.3.4 Create a scatterplot for Cluster 1, 2 and 3 with labels

```{r}
ggplot(genus_cluster1, aes(x = genus_cluster1$`pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.1`, y = genus_cluster1$`pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.2`, label = rownames(genus_cluster1))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(genus_cluster1)), vjust = -0.5, size =3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster with UTI samples (Cluster 1)")

ggplot(genus_cluster2, aes(x = genus_cluster2$`pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.1`, y = genus_cluster2$`pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.2`, label = rownames(genus_cluster2))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(genus_cluster2)), vjust = -0.5, size =3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster with UTI samples (Cluster 2)")

ggplot(genus_cluster3, aes(x = genus_cluster3$`pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.1`, y = genus_cluster3$`pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.2`, label = rownames(genus_cluster3))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(genus_cluster3)), vjust = -0.5, size =3) +
  labs(title = "Cluster with UTI samples (Cluster 3)")

```


### 24.3.5 Boxplots for the three clusters

```{r}
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Cluster = sample_list..kmeans_genus.cluster.)
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Axis1 = `pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.1`)
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance <- sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance %>%
  rename(Axis2 = `pcoa_coords_allcontaminants$Axis.2`)
  
sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$Cluster <- as.factor(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance$Cluster)

wilc_results <- list()
bacteria_plots <- list()
pdf('KWPlots_AllContaminants.pdf')

# Perform the tests and generate plots
for(i in 4:ncol(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance)) {
    print(i)

    # Extract data for all clusters
    data_cluster1 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster1[, i])
    data_cluster2 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster2[, i])
    data_cluster3 <- as.numeric(genus_cluster3[, i])

    # Combine data for Kruskal-Wallis test
    all_data <- c(data_cluster1, data_cluster2, data_cluster3)
    group_labels <- c(rep("Cluster1", length(data_cluster1)), 
                      rep("Cluster2", length(data_cluster2)), 
                      rep("Cluster3", length(data_cluster3)))

    # Skip if any group has no variation or insufficient data
    if (length(unique(all_data[!is.na(all_data)])) <= 1) {
        warning(paste("Skipping column", i, "- insufficient data or no variation."))
        next
    }

    # Perform Kruskal-Wallis test
    kruskal_result <- tryCatch(kruskal.test(all_data ~ group_labels), 
                               error = function(e) NA)

    # Check if Kruskal-Wallis test returned valid results
    if (is.na(kruskal_result$p.value)) {
        warning(paste("Skipping column", i, "- Kruskal-Wallis test failed."))
        next
    }

    wilc_results[[paste0("kruskal_", i)]] <- kruskal_result$p.value

    # Post-hoc pairwise Wilcoxon test if Kruskal-Wallis is significant
    if (kruskal_result$p.value < 0.05) {
        pairwise_results <- pairwise.wilcox.test(all_data, group_labels, 
                                                 p.adjust.method = "BH", exact = FALSE)
        wilc_results[[paste0("pairwise_", i)]] <- pairwise_results$p.value
        
        # Calculate medians for plotting
        clus1_median <- median(data_cluster1, na.rm = TRUE)
        clus2_median <- median(data_cluster2, na.rm = TRUE)
        clus3_median <- median(data_cluster3, na.rm = TRUE)

        # Generate boxplot with statistical annotations only if significant
        bacteria_plots[[i]] <- ggplot(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance, aes(y = sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance[, i], x = Cluster)) +
            geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
            geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0) +
            theme_minimal() +
            scale_y_sqrt() +
            labs(y = paste0(colnames(sample_cluster_coords_genus_abundance)[i])) +
            stat_compare_means(method = "kruskal.test", label.y = max(all_data, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1)

        print(bacteria_plots[[i]])
    }
}

dev.off()

```

### 24.3.6 Combined Dataframe with Abundance data and cluster data
```{r}
combined_data_allcontaminants.rel <- merge(hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_allcontaminants, 
                                      salter_allcontaminants.rel, by = "row.names")

rownames(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel) <- combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$Row.names
combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$Row.names <- NULL

```

### 24.3.7 Encode Progression as a factor
```{r}
combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$progressed <- as.factor(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$progressed)
```

### 24.3.8 Create a Survival Object

```{r}
survival_obj_abbs_allcontaminants.rel <- Surv(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free,
                                         combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$progressed)
```

### 24.3.9 Run Boruta on the Survival Object

```{r}
boruta_allcontaminants_survfit.rel <- Boruta(salter_allcontaminants.rel, survival_obj_abbs_allcontaminants.rel, pValue = 0.05, maxRuns = 10000, doTrace = 2)

boruta_allcontaminants_finaldecision.rel <- as.data.frame(boruta_allcontaminants_survfit.rel$finalDecision)

write.csv(file = "Boruta_RelAb_AllContaminants_FinalDecision.csv", boruta_allcontaminants_finaldecision.rel)
```
Acinetobacter has been confirmed in both consensus and all contaminant dataframes. All contaminant also confirms Enterococcus and is tentative on Peptoniphilus_E.
Interestingly, Enterococcus was also tentative on the presence-absence in the all contaminant dataframe so maybe there is something there?


# 25. Generation of KM Curves for Each Dataframe

## 25.1 No Contaminant Dataframe

The organisms found significant here were Enterococcus and Peptoniphilus_E

### 25.2.1 Enterococcus

```{r}
enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel <- data.frame(Overall_Progression_free = c(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free), 
                                              Progressed = c(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$progressed),
                                              Enterococcus = c(combined_data_nocontaminants$Enterococcus))
enterococcus_rownames <- c(rownames(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel))
rownames(enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel) <- enterococcus_rownames

enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Progressed <- as.numeric(enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

1 - No progression 2- Progressed
#### 25.2.1.1 Create a Surv object

```{r}
enterococcus_nocontaminants_surv_object.rel <- Surv(time = enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Overall_Progression_free, 
                                          event = enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

#### 25.2.1.2 Fit the Kaplan-Meier survival curve by Winkia status

```{r}
enterococcus_nocontaminants_fit.rel <- survfit(enterococcus_nocontaminants_surv_object.rel ~ Enterococcus, data = enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel)
```

#### 25.2.1.3 Plot the survival curve

```{r}
ggsurvplot(enterococcus_nocontaminants_fit.rel, data = enterococcus_subset_nocontaminants.rel, 
           pval = TRUE, 
           conf.int = TRUE,
           legend.title = "Enterococcus Status",
           legend.labs = c("Absent (0)", "Present (1)"),
           xlab = "Progression-Free Days",
           ylab = "Survival Probability",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

### 25.2.1 Peptoniphilus_E

```{r}
peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel <- data.frame(Overall_Progression_free = c(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free), 
                                              Progressed = c(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel$progressed),
                                              Peptoniphilus_E = c(combined_data_nocontaminants$Peptoniphilus_E))
peptoniphilus_e_rownames <- c(rownames(combined_data_nocontaminants.rel))
rownames(peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel) <- peptoniphilus_e_rownames

peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Progressed <- as.numeric(peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

1 - No progression 2- Progressed
#### 25.2.1.1 Create a Surv object

```{r}
peptoniphilus_e_nocontaminants_surv_object.rel <- Surv(time = peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Overall_Progression_free, 
                                          event = peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

#### 25.2.1.2 Fit the Kaplan-Meier survival curve by Winkia status

```{r}
peptoniphilus_e_nocontaminants_fit.rel <- survfit(peptoniphilus_e_nocontaminants_surv_object.rel ~ Peptoniphilus_E, data = peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel)
```

#### 25.2.1.3 Plot the survival curve

```{r}
ggsurvplot(peptoniphilus_e_nocontaminants_fit.rel, data = peptoniphilus_e_subset_nocontaminants.rel, 
           pval = TRUE, 
           conf.int = TRUE,
           legend.title = "Peptoniphilus_E Status",
           legend.labs = c("Absent (0)", "Present (1)"),
           xlab = "Progression-Free Days",
           ylab = "Survival Probability",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

## 25.2 Consensus Dataframe

The organisms found significant here was Acinetobacter

### 25.2.1 Acinetobacter

```{r}
acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel <- data.frame(Overall_Progression_free = c(combined_data_consensus.rel$overall_progression_free), 
                                              Progressed = c(combined_data_consensus.rel$progressed),
                                              Acinetobacter = c(combined_data_consensus$Acinetobacter))
acinetobacter_rownames <- c(rownames(combined_data_consensus.rel))
rownames(acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel) <- acinetobacter_rownames

acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel$Progressed <- as.numeric(acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel$Progressed)
```

1 - No progression 2- Progressed
#### 25.2.1.1 Create a Surv object

```{r}
acinetobacter_consensus_surv_object.rel <- Surv(time = acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel$Overall_Progression_free, 
                                          event = acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel$Progressed)
```

#### 25.2.1.2 Fit the Kaplan-Meier survival curve by Winkia status

```{r}
acinetobacter_consensus_fit.rel <- survfit(acinetobacter_consensus_surv_object.rel ~ Acinetobacter, data = acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel)
```

#### 25.2.1.3 Plot the survival curve

```{r}
ggsurvplot(acinetobacter_consensus_fit.rel, data = acinetobacter_subset_consensus.rel, 
           pval = TRUE, 
           conf.int = TRUE,
           legend.title = "Acinetobacter Status",
           legend.labs = c("Absent (0)", "Present (1)"),
           xlab = "Progression-Free Days",
           ylab = "Survival Probability",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

## 25.3 All Contaminant Dataframe

The organisms found significant here were Enterococcus and Acinetobacter with Peptoniphilus_E being tentative

### 25.3.1 Enterococcus

```{r}
enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel <- data.frame(Overall_Progression_free = c(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free), 
                                              Progressed = c(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$progressed),
                                              Enterococcus = c(combined_data_allcontaminants$Enterococcus))
enterococcus_rownames <- c(rownames(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel))
rownames(enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel) <- enterococcus_rownames

enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed <- as.numeric(enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

1 - No progression 2- Progressed
#### 25.3.1.1 Create a Surv object

```{r}
enterococcus_allcontaminants_surv_object.rel <- Surv(time = enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Overall_Progression_free, 
                                          event = enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

#### 25.3.1.2 Fit the Kaplan-Meier survival curve by Winkia status

```{r}
enterococcus_allcontaminants_fit.rel <- survfit(enterococcus_allcontaminants_surv_object.rel ~ Enterococcus, data = enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel)
```

#### 25.3.1.3 Plot the survival curve

```{r}
ggsurvplot(enterococcus_allcontaminants_fit.rel, data = enterococcus_subset_allcontaminants.rel, 
           pval = TRUE, 
           conf.int = TRUE,
           legend.title = "Enterococcus Status",
           legend.labs = c("Absent (0)", "Present (1)"),
           xlab = "Progression-Free Days",
           ylab = "Survival Probability",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

### 25.3.2 Acinetobacter

```{r}
acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel <- data.frame(Overall_Progression_free = c(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free), 
                                              Progressed = c(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$progressed),
                                              Acinetobacter = c(combined_data_allcontaminants$Acinetobacter))
acinetobacter_rownames <- c(rownames(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel))
rownames(acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel) <- acinetobacter_rownames

acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed <- as.numeric(acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

1 - No progression 2- Progressed
#### 25.3.2.1 Create a Surv object

```{r}
acinetobacter_allcontaminants_surv_object.rel <- Surv(time = acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Overall_Progression_free, 
                                          event = acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

#### 25.3.2.2 Fit the Kaplan-Meier survival curve by Winkia status

```{r}
acinetobacter_allcontaminants_fit.rel <- survfit(acinetobacter_allcontaminants_surv_object.rel ~ Acinetobacter, data = acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel)
```

#### 25.3.2.3 Plot the survival curve

```{r}
ggsurvplot(acinetobacter_allcontaminants_fit.rel, data = acinetobacter_subset_allcontaminants.rel, 
           pval = TRUE, 
           conf.int = TRUE,
           legend.title = "Acinetobacter Status",
           legend.labs = c("Absent (0)", "Present (1)"),
           xlab = "Progression-Free Days",
           ylab = "Survival Probability",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

### 25.3.3 Peptoniphilus_E

```{r}
peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel <- data.frame(Overall_Progression_free = c(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$overall_progression_free), 
                                              Progressed = c(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel$progressed),
                                              Peptoniphilus_E = c(combined_data_allcontaminants$Peptoniphilus_E))
peptoniphilus_e_rownames <- c(rownames(combined_data_allcontaminants.rel))
rownames(peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel) <- peptoniphilus_e_rownames

peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed <- as.numeric(peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

1 - No progression 2- Progressed
#### 25.3.3.1 Create a Surv object

```{r}
peptoniphilus_e_allcontaminants_surv_object.rel <- Surv(time = peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Overall_Progression_free, 
                                          event = peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel$Progressed)
```

#### 25.3.3.2 Fit the Kaplan-Meier survival curve by Winkia status

```{r}
peptoniphilus_e_allcontaminants_fit.rel <- survfit(peptoniphilus_e_allcontaminants_surv_object.rel ~ Peptoniphilus_E, data = peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel)
```

#### 25.3.3.3 Plot the survival curve

```{r}
ggsurvplot(peptoniphilus_e_allcontaminants_fit.rel, data = peptoniphilus_e_subset_allcontaminants.rel, 
           pval = TRUE, 
           conf.int = TRUE,
           legend.title = "Peptoniphilus_E Status",
           legend.labs = c("Absent (0)", "Present (1)"),
           xlab = "Progression-Free Days",
           ylab = "Survival Probability",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```


# 26. CORRELATION ANALYSIS

I have tried to look into doing correlation analysis using some packages that look at mutual exclusivity and cooccurence in gene mutation studies. However, the problem is that the gene mutation packages require the data to be in a specific file format (MAF to be precise) and that means the data is in a specific format which cannot be done with microbiome data. I could try to replicate the individual mathematical basis that the gene studies do and try to do it individually. 

However, to start with I have just decided to do something simple in terms of correlation.

Also should I do this with only cancer samples?

## 26.1 Use the relative abundance dataframes generated in 6.1.1

```{r}
salter_nocontaminants_corr <- salter_nocontaminants.rel
salter_consensus_corr <- salter_consensus.rel
salter_allcontaminants_corr <- salter_allcontaminants.rel
```


## 26.2 Remove rare taxa

For the purposes of this, we will not apply any specific percentage threshold but instead apply the same criteria that I did based on the number of samples i.e. present in at least 2 samples

```{r}
salter_nocontaminants_corr <- salter_nocontaminants_corr[, colSums(salter_nocontaminants_corr >0) >= 2]

salter_consensus_corr <- salter_consensus_corr[, colSums(salter_consensus_corr >0) >= 2]

salter_allcontaminants_corr <- salter_allcontaminants_corr[, colSums(salter_allcontaminants_corr >0) >= 2]
```


## 26.3 iGraph

### 26.3.1 Compute pairwise correlations

```{r}
cor_matrix_nocontaminants <- cor(salter_nocontaminants_corr, method = "spearman")
cor_test_nocontaminants <- rcorr(as.matrix(salter_nocontaminants_corr), type = "spearman")
cor_pvals_nocontaminants <- cor_test_nocontaminants$P

cor_matrix_consensus <- cor(salter_consensus_corr, method = "spearman")
cor_test_consensus <- rcorr(as.matrix(salter_consensus_corr), type = "spearman")
cor_pvals_consensus <- cor_test_consensus$P

cor_matrix_allcontaminants <- cor(salter_allcontaminants_corr, method = "spearman")
cor_test_allcontaminants <- rcorr(as.matrix(salter_allcontaminants_corr), type = "spearman")
cor_pvals_allcontaminants <- cor_test_allcontaminants$P
```

### 26.3.2 Identify significant correlations

Do multiple testing correction here with FDR as well

```{r}
cor_pvals_adj_nocontaminants <- p.adjust(cor_pvals_nocontaminants, method = "fdr")
cor_pvals_adj_consensus <- p.adjust(cor_pvals_consensus, method = "fdr")
cor_pvals_adj_allcontaminants <- p.adjust(cor_pvals_allcontaminants, method = "fdr")

significant_cor_nocontaminants <- cor_matrix_nocontaminants
significant_cor_consensus <- cor_matrix_consensus
significant_cor_allcontaminants <- cor_matrix_allcontaminants

significant_cor_nocontaminants[abs(cor_pvals_adj_nocontaminants) > 0.05] <- NA
significant_cor_consensus[abs(cor_pvals_adj_consensus) > 0.05] <- NA
significant_cor_allcontaminants[abs(cor_pvals_adj_allcontaminants) > 0.05] <- NA
```

### 26.3.3 Take correlations >0.6
Convert the significant_cor to a dataframe and then pivot to long form and then extract only the correlations >0.6

```{r}
significant_cor_nocontaminants.df <- as.data.frame(significant_cor_nocontaminants)
significant_cor_consensus.df <- as.data.frame(significant_cor_consensus)
significant_cor_allcontaminants.df <- as.data.frame(significant_cor_allcontaminants)

significant_cor_nocontaminants.long <- as.data.frame(as.table(significant_cor_nocontaminants))
significant_cor_consensus.long <- as.data.frame(as.table(significant_cor_consensus))
significant_cor_allcontaminants.long <- as.data.frame(as.table(significant_cor_allcontaminants))

colnames(significant_cor_nocontaminants.long) <- c("Bacteria1_NC", "Bacteria2_NC", "Correlation_NC")
colnames(significant_cor_consensus.long) <- c("Bacteria1_Con", "Bacteria2_Con", "Correlation_Con")
colnames(significant_cor_allcontaminants.long) <- c("Bacteria1_AC", "Bacteria2_AC", "Correlation_AC")

cor_06_list_nocontaminants <- significant_cor_nocontaminants.long %>%
  filter(Bacteria1_NC != Bacteria2_NC) %>%
  filter(abs(Correlation_NC) >= 0.6)
cor_06_list_consensus <- significant_cor_consensus.long %>%
  filter(Bacteria1_Con != Bacteria2_Con) %>%
  filter(abs(Correlation_Con) >= 0.6)
cor_06_list_allcontaminants <- significant_cor_allcontaminants.long %>%
  filter(Bacteria1_AC != Bacteria2_AC) %>%
  filter(abs(Correlation_AC) >= 0.6)

write.csv(file = "Correlation_List_NC_HILA.csv", cor_06_list_nocontaminants)
write.csv(file = "Correlation_List_Con_HILA.csv", cor_06_list_consensus)
write.csv(file = "Correlation_List_AC_HILA.csv", cor_06_list_allcontaminants)

```

### 26.3.4 Represent Graphically

```{r}
graph_nocontaminants <- graph_from_data_frame(cor_06_list_nocontaminants, directed = FALSE)
graph_consensus <- graph_from_data_frame(cor_06_list_consensus, directed = FALSE)
graph_allcontaminants <- graph_from_data_frame(cor_06_list_allcontaminants, directed = FALSE)

E(graph_nocontaminants)$weight <- cor_06_list_nocontaminants$Correlation_NC
E(graph_consensus)$weight <- cor_06_list_consensus$Correlation_Con
E(graph_allcontaminants)$weight <- cor_06_list_allcontaminants$Correlation_AC

layout_fr_nocontaminants <- layout_with_kk(graph_nocontaminants)
layout_fr_consensus <- layout_with_kk(graph_consensus)
layout_fr_allcontaminants <- layout_with_kk(graph_allcontaminants)

plot(graph_nocontaminants, 
     layout = layout_fr_nocontaminants, 
     edge.width = E(graph_nocontaminants)$weight*5,
     edge.color = ifelse(E(graph_nocontaminants)$weight > 0, "blue", "red"),
     vertex.size = 10, vertex.label.cex = 0.5, 
     main = "Bacterial Correlation Network - No Contaminants")

plot(graph_consensus, 
     layout= layout_fr_consensus,
     edge.width = E(graph_consensus)$weight * 5,
     edge.color = ifelse(E(graph_nocontaminants)$weight > 0, "blue", "red"),
     vertex.size = 10, vertex.label.cex = 0.5, 
     main = "Bacterial Correlation Network - Consensus")

plot(graph_consensus, 
     layout = layout_fr_allcontaminants, 
     edge.width = E(graph_consensus)$weight * 5, 
     edge.color = ifelse(E(graph_consensus)$weight > 0, "blue", "red"),
     vertex.size = 10, vertex.label.cex = 0.5, 
     main = "Bacterial Correlation Network - All Contaminants")

```
## 26.4 Bubble Plot

```{r}
colnames(cor_06_list_nocontaminants) <- c("Genus_1", "Genus_2", "Correlation")
colnames(cor_06_list_consensus) <- c("Genus_1", "Genus_2", "Correlation")
colnames(cor_06_list_allcontaminants) <- c("Genus_1", "Genus_2", "Correlation")

ggplot(cor_06_list_nocontaminants, aes(x=Genus_1, y=Genus_2, 
                                       color = Correlation)) +
  geom_point()+
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3,10)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "red", high = "blue", mid = "green", midpoint=0.8)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 3),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 3))
  labs(title = "Bacterial Correlation - No contaminants", 
       x = "Bacteria 1", y = "Bacteria 2")
  
ggplot(cor_06_list_consensus, aes(x=Genus_1, y=Genus_2, color = Correlation)) +
  geom_point()+
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3,10)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "red", high = "blue", mid = "green", midpoint=0.8)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 3),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 3))
  labs(title = "Bacterial Correlation - Consensus", 
       x = "Bacteria 1", y = "Bacteria 2")
  
  
ggplot(cor_06_list_allcontaminants, aes(x=Genus_1, y=Genus_2, color = Correlation)) +
  geom_point()+
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3,10)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "red", high = "blue", mid = "green", midpoint=0.8)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 3),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 3))
  labs(title = "Bacterial Correlation - All contaminants", 
       x = "Bacteria 1", y = "Bacteria 2")

```

Check for correlations of ABBS species with other bacteria so only select ABBS out of the significant species list that you have got

```{r}
abbs_list <- c("Ezakiella", "Fenollaria", "Anaerococcus", "Porphyromonas_A", "Porphyromonas", "Peptoniphilus_C", "Peptoniphilus_A", "Fusobacterium", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Peptoniphilus_D")

cor_06_list_nocontaminants_abbs <- cor_06_list_nocontaminants %>%
  filter(Genus_1 %in% abbs_list)
cor_06_list_consensus_abbs <- cor_06_list_consensus %>%
  filter(Genus_1 %in% abbs_list)
cor_06_list_allcontaminants_abbs <- cor_06_list_allcontaminants %>%
  filter(Genus_1%in% abbs_list)

write.csv(file = "Cor_06_List_NoContaminants_ABBS.csv", cor_06_list_nocontaminants_abbs)
write.csv(file = "Cor_06_List_Consensus_ABBS.csv", cor_06_list_consensus_abbs)
write.csv(file = "Cor_06_List_AllContaminants_ABBS.csv", cor_06_list_allcontaminants_abbs)

plot_corr <- function(data, title_text){

  ggplot(data,
         aes(x = Genus_1,
             y = Genus_2,
             colour = Correlation,
             size = abs(Correlation))) +

    geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +

    geom_text(aes(label = round(Correlation, 2)),
              size = 5,
              color = "black",
              vjust = -0.8,
              hjust = 0.5,
              fontface = "bold") +

    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6)) +

    scale_color_gradient2(
      low = "red",
      mid = "green",
      high = "blue",
      midpoint = 0.75
    ) +

    theme_minimal() +

    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                 hjust = 1,
                                 size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +

    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "Bacteria 1",
      y = "Bacteria 2",
      size = "|Correlation|"
    )
}

p1 <- plot_corr(cor_06_list_nocontaminants_abbs,
          "Bacterial Correlation - No contaminants")

p2 <- plot_corr(cor_06_list_consensus_abbs,
          "Bacterial Correlation - Consensus")

p3 <- plot_corr(cor_06_list_allcontaminants_abbs,
          "Bacterial Correlation - All contaminants")

ggsave("cor_no_contaminants.png", p1, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("cor_consensus.png", p2, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("cor_all_contaminants.png", p3, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```


# Currently Working on this (RELATIVE ABUNDANCE) ----

## No contaminant dataframe

``` {r}
salter_nocontaminants.rel.2 <- salter_nocontaminants.rel[, colSums(salter_nocontaminants.rel >0) >= 2]
salter_consensus.rel.2 <- salter_consensus.rel[, colSums(salter_consensus.rel >0) >= 2]
salter_allcontaminants.rel.2 <- salter_allcontaminants.rel[, colSums(salter_allcontaminants.rel >0) >= 2]

merged_nocontaminants.rel <- merge(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2, hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_nocontaminants, by = "row.names")
colnames(merged_nocontaminants.rel)[1] <- "Sample"
genus_cols <- colnames(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2)

quartile_nocontaminants <- merged_nocontaminants.rel %>%
pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(genus_cols),
    names_to = "Genus",          
    values_to = "Abundance"      
  )

pdf("KMCurves_NC_Relab_Quartiles.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
# Kaplan-Meier Survival Analysis
for (genus in unique(quartile_nocontaminants$Genus)) {
  genus_data <- quartile_nocontaminants %>% filter(Genus == genus)
 # Ensure 'Abundance' exists in the filtered data
  if ("Abundance" %in% colnames(genus_data)) {
    # Create a Surv object
    surv_obj <- Surv(genus_data$overall_progression_free, genus_data$progressed)
    
    # Add jitter to abundance if values are identical (optional)
    genus_data <- genus_data %>%
      mutate(Abundance = Abundance + runif(n(), min = -1e-6, max = 1e-6))
    
    # Compute quantile breaks and ensure they are unique
    quantile_breaks <- unique(quantile(genus_data$Abundance, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE))
    
    # Ensure labels match the number of intervals
    n_intervals <- length(quantile_breaks) - 1 # Number of intervals is one less than the number of breaks
    quartile_labels <- paste0("Q", seq_len(n_intervals)) # Dynamically generate labels (Q1, Q2, ...)

    # Divide Abundance into quartiles
    genus_data <- genus_data %>%
      mutate(Quartile = cut(Abundance,
                            breaks = quantile_breaks,
                            include.lowest = TRUE,
                            labels = quartile_labels))
    
    # Fit survival curves
    fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Quartile, data = genus_data)
    
   # Plot and save each KM curve
    plot <- ggsurvplot(
      fit,
      data = genus_data,
      title = paste("KM Curve for", genus),
      xlab = "Time",
      ylab = "Survival Probability",
      legend.title = "Quartile",
      pval = TRUE # Add p-value
    )
    
    print(plot) # Print plot to the PDF
  }
}
dev.off()
```

## Consensus Dataframe

```{r}
merged_consensus.rel <- merge(salter_consensus.rel.2, hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_consensus, by = "row.names")
colnames(merged_consensus.rel)[1] <- "Sample"
genus_cols <- colnames(salter_consensus.rel.2)

quartile_consensus <- merged_consensus.rel %>%
pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(genus_cols),
    names_to = "Genus",          
    values_to = "Abundance"      
  )

pdf("KMCurves_Con_Relab_Quartiles.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
# Kaplan-Meier Survival Analysis
for (genus in unique(quartile_consensus$Genus)) {
  genus_data <- quartile_consensus %>% filter(Genus == genus)
 # Ensure 'Abundance' exists in the filtered data
  if ("Abundance" %in% colnames(genus_data)) {
    # Create a Surv object
    surv_obj <- Surv(genus_data$overall_progression_free, genus_data$progressed)
    
    # Add jitter to abundance if values are identical (optional)
    genus_data <- genus_data %>%
      mutate(Abundance = Abundance + runif(n(), min = -1e-6, max = 1e-6))
    
    # Compute quantile breaks and ensure they are unique
    quantile_breaks <- unique(quantile(genus_data$Abundance, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE))
    
    # Ensure labels match the number of intervals
    n_intervals <- length(quantile_breaks) - 1 # Number of intervals is one less than the number of breaks
    quartile_labels <- paste0("Q", seq_len(n_intervals)) # Dynamically generate labels (Q1, Q2, ...)

    # Divide Abundance into quartiles
    genus_data <- genus_data %>%
      mutate(Quartile = cut(Abundance,
                            breaks = quantile_breaks,
                            include.lowest = TRUE,
                            labels = quartile_labels))
    
    # Fit survival curves
    fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Quartile, data = genus_data)
    
   # Plot and save each KM curve
    plot <- ggsurvplot(
      fit,
      data = genus_data,
      title = paste("KM Curve for", genus),
      xlab = "Time",
      ylab = "Survival Probability",
      legend.title = "Quartile",
      pval = TRUE # Add p-value
    )
    
    print(plot) # Print plot to the PDF
  }
}
dev.off()
```

## All Contaminants Dataframe

```{r}
merged_allcontaminants.rel <- merge(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2, hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_allcontaminants, by = "row.names")
colnames(merged_allcontaminants.rel)[1] <- "Sample"
genus_cols <- colnames(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2)

quartile_allcontaminants <- merged_allcontaminants.rel %>%
pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(genus_cols),
    names_to = "Genus",          
    values_to = "Abundance"      
  )
pdf("KMCurves_AC_Relab_Quartiles.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
# Kaplan-Meier Survival Analysis
for (genus in unique(quartile_allcontaminants$Genus)) {
  genus_data <- quartile_allcontaminants %>% filter(Genus == genus)
 # Ensure 'Abundance' exists in the filtered data
  if ("Abundance" %in% colnames(genus_data)) {
    # Create a Surv object
    surv_obj <- Surv(genus_data$overall_progression_free, genus_data$progressed)
    
    # Add jitter to abundance if values are identical (optional)
    genus_data <- genus_data %>%
      mutate(Abundance = Abundance + runif(n(), min = -1e-6, max = 1e-6))
    
    # Compute quantile breaks and ensure they are unique
    quantile_breaks <- unique(quantile(genus_data$Abundance, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE))
    
    # Ensure labels match the number of intervals
    n_intervals <- length(quantile_breaks) - 1 # Number of intervals is one less than the number of breaks
    quartile_labels <- paste0("Q", seq_len(n_intervals)) # Dynamically generate labels (Q1, Q2, ...)

    # Divide Abundance into quartiles
    genus_data <- genus_data %>%
      mutate(Quartile = cut(Abundance,
                            breaks = quantile_breaks,
                            include.lowest = TRUE,
                            labels = quartile_labels))
    
    # Fit survival curves
    fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ Quartile, data = genus_data)
    
   # Plot and save each KM curve
    plot <- ggsurvplot(
      fit,
      data = genus_data,
      title = paste("KM Curve for", genus),
      xlab = "Time",
      ylab = "Survival Probability",
      legend.title = "Quartile",
      pval = TRUE # Add p-value
    )
    
    print(plot) # Print plot to the PDF
  }
}
dev.off()
```

# Scatterplots with Urinicoccus and ABBS

The ABBS that Urinicoccus occurs with are Porphyromonas_A, Ezakiella, Porphyromonas and Peptoniphilus_E

Extract only the relevant columns from the relative abundance dataframe

## No contaminant Dataframe
```{r}
urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel <- salter_nocontaminants.rel
urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel <- urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel %>%
  select("Porphyromonas_A", "Porphyromonas", "Ezakiella", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Urinicoccus")

urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel_long <- pivot_longer(urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel, cols = 1:4, names_to = "Genera", values_to = "Abundance")

ggplot(urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel_long, aes(x = Urinicoccus, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~Genera) +
  labs(title = "Relationship of Urinicoccus with Other ABBS Genera",
       x = "Urinicoccus Abundance",
       y = "Relative Abundance of Other ABBS Genera") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Plot each scatterplot separately

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Porphyromonas_A, main = "Urinicoccus vs Porphyromonas_A", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Porphyromonas_A", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Porphyromonas, main = "Urinicoccus vs Porphyromonas", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Porphyromonas", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Ezakiella, main = "Urinicoccus vs Ezakiella", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Ezakiella", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_nocontaminants.rel$Peptoniphilus_E, main = "Urinicoccus vs Peptoniphilus_E", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Peptoniphilus_E", pch = 16, col = "blue")

```

## Consensus Dataframe
```{r}
urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel <- salter_consensus.rel
urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel <- urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel %>%
  select("Porphyromonas_A", "Porphyromonas", "Ezakiella", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Urinicoccus")

urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel_long <- pivot_longer(urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel, cols = 1:4, names_to = "Genera", values_to = "Abundance")

ggplot(urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel_long, aes(x = Urinicoccus, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~Genera) +
  labs(title = "Relationship of Urinicoccus with Other ABBS Genera",
       x = "Urinicoccus Abundance",
       y = "Relative Abundance of Other ABBS Genera") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# Plot each scatterplot separately

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Porphyromonas_A, main = "Urinicoccus vs Porphyromonas_A", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Porphyromonas_A", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Porphyromonas, main = "Urinicoccus vs Porphyromonas", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Porphyromonas", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Ezakiella, main = "Urinicoccus vs Ezakiella", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Ezakiella", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_consensus.rel$Peptoniphilus_E, main = "Urinicoccus vs Peptoniphilus_E", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Peptoniphilus_E", pch = 16, col = "blue")

```

## All Contaminants Dataframe

```{r}
urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel <- salter_allcontaminants.rel
urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel <- urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel %>%
  select("Porphyromonas_A", "Porphyromonas", "Ezakiella", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Urinicoccus")

urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel_long <- pivot_longer(urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel, cols = 1:4, names_to = "Genera", values_to = "Abundance")

ggplot(urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel_long, aes(x = Urinicoccus, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~Genera) +
  labs(title = "Relationship of Urinicoccus with Other ABBS Genera",
       x = "Urinicoccus Abundance",
       y = "Relative Abundance of Other ABBS Genera") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
# Plot each scatterplot separately

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Porphyromonas_A, main = "Urinicoccus vs Porphyromonas_A", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Porphyromonas_A", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Porphyromonas, main = "Urinicoccus vs Porphyromonas", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Porphyromonas", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Ezakiella, main = "Urinicoccus vs Ezakiella", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Ezakiella", pch = 16, col = "blue")

plot(urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Urinicoccus, urinicoccus_scatplot_allcontaminants.rel$Peptoniphilus_E, main = "Urinicoccus vs Peptoniphilus_E", xlab = "Urinicoccus", ylab = "Peptoniphilus_E", pch = 16, col = "blue")

```
Extract the percentage relative abundance in cancer samples for just these five genera in all three dataframes

```{r}
abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants <- salter_nocontaminants.rel %>%
  select("Urinicoccus", "Porphyromonas", "Porphyromonas_A", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Ezakiella")
abbs_urinicoccus_consensus <- salter_consensus.rel %>%
  select("Urinicoccus", "Porphyromonas", "Porphyromonas_A", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Ezakiella")
abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants <- salter_allcontaminants.rel %>%
  select("Urinicoccus", "Porphyromonas", "Porphyromonas_A", "Peptoniphilus_E", "Ezakiella")
```

Convert anything >0 to present

```{r}
abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA<- abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~1 * (. > 0))
abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA<- abbs_urinicoccus_consensus %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~1 * (. > 0))
abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA<- abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~1 * (. > 0))
```

Create contingency tables and get the output

```{r}
#No contaminant Dataframe
nocontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Porphyromonas_A, abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(nocontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA) <- list(
  "Presence of Porphyromonas_A" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
nocontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA

nocontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonas <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Porphyromonas, abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(nocontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonas) <- list(
  "Presence of Porphyromonas" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
nocontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonas

nocontaminants_urinicoccus_ezakiella <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Ezakiella, abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(nocontaminants_urinicoccus_ezakiella) <- list(
  "Presence of Ezakiella" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
nocontaminants_urinicoccus_ezakiella

nocontaminants_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Peptoniphilus_E, abbs_urinicoccus_nocontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(nocontaminants_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e) <- list(
  "Presence of Peptoniphilus_E" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
nocontaminants_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e 

#Consensus
consensus_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Porphyromonas_A, abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(consensus_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA) <- list(
  "Presence of Porphyromonas_A" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
consensus_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA

consensus_urinicoccus_porphyromonas <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Porphyromonas, abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(consensus_urinicoccus_porphyromonas) <- list(
  "Presence of Porphyromonas" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
consensus_urinicoccus_porphyromonas

consensus_urinicoccus_ezakiella <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Ezakiella, abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(consensus_urinicoccus_ezakiella) <- list(
  "Presence of Ezakiella" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
consensus_urinicoccus_ezakiella

consensus_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Peptoniphilus_E, abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(consensus_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e) <- list(
  "Presence of Peptoniphilus_E" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
consensus_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e 


#All Contaminants
allcontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Porphyromonas_A, abbs_urinicoccus_consensus.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(allcontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA) <- list(
  "Presence of Porphyromonas_A" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
allcontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonasA

allcontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonas <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Porphyromonas, abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(allcontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonas) <- list(
  "Presence of Porphyromonas" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
allcontaminants_urinicoccus_porphyromonas

allcontaminants_urinicoccus_ezakiella <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Ezakiella, abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(allcontaminants_urinicoccus_ezakiella) <- list(
  "Presence of Ezakiella" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
allcontaminants_urinicoccus_ezakiella

allcontaminants_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e <- table(abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Peptoniphilus_E, abbs_urinicoccus_allcontaminants.rel.PA$Urinicoccus)
dimnames(allcontaminants_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e) <- list(
  "Presence of Peptoniphilus_E" = c("Absent", "Present"),
  "Presence of Urinicoccus" = c("Absent", "Present")
)
allcontaminants_urinicoccus_peptoniphilus_e 
```

# Correlation for Presence-Absence

```{r}
# Filter columns with at least 2 occurrences
salter_nocontaminants.rel.2 <- salter_nocontaminants.rel[, colSums(salter_nocontaminants.rel > 0) >= 2]
salter_consensus.rel.2 <- salter_consensus.rel[, colSums(salter_consensus.rel > 0) >= 2]
salter_allcontaminants.rel.2 <- salter_allcontaminants.rel[, colSums(salter_allcontaminants.rel > 0) >= 2]

# Convert to Presence-Absence (PA) format
salter_nocontaminants.rel.2.PA <- salter_nocontaminants.rel.2 %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~1 * (. > 0))
salter_consensus.rel.2.PA <- salter_consensus.rel.2 %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~1 * (. > 0))
salter_allcontaminants.rel.2.PA <- salter_allcontaminants.rel.2 %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~1 * (. > 0))

# Function to perform Chi-square tests for a given dataset
perform_chi_square <- function(data) {
  results <- list()
  
  if (ncol(data) > 1) {  # Ensure there are at least two genera to compare
    for (i in 1:(ncol(data) - 1)) {
      for (j in (i + 1):ncol(data)) {
        # Create contingency table
        contingency_table <- table(data[[i]], data[[j]])
        
        # Ensure the table is at least 2x2
        if (nrow(contingency_table) == 2 && ncol(contingency_table) == 2) {
          # Perform Chi-square test
          test_result <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
          
          # Store results
          results[[paste(colnames(data)[i], colnames(data)[j], sep = "_vs_")]] <- list(
            p_value = test_result$p.value,
            statistic = test_result$statistic,
            observed = contingency_table
          )
        } else {
          cat("Skipping:", colnames(data)[i], "vs", colnames(data)[j], "- Not a 2x2 table\n")
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Convert results into a data frame
    results_df <- tibble(
      Comparison = names(results),
      p_value = sapply(results, function(x) x$p_value),
      statistic = sapply(results, function(x) x$statistic)
    ) %>% arrange(p_value)
    
    return(results_df)
  } else {
    cat("Not enough genera for pairwise comparison.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Run Chi-square tests on each dataset
nocontaminants_results <- perform_chi_square(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2.PA)
consensus_results <- perform_chi_square(salter_consensus.rel.2.PA)
allcontaminants_results <- perform_chi_square(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2.PA)

nocontaminants_results <- nocontaminants_results %>%
  filter(p_value < 0.05)
consensus_results <- consensus_results %>%
  filter(p_value < 0.05)
allcontaminants_results <- allcontaminants_results %>%
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# Print or save results
write.csv(file = "NoContaminants_ChiSq.csv", nocontaminants_results)
write.csv(file = "Consensus_ChiSq.csv", consensus_results)
write.csv(file = "AllContaminants_ChiSq.csv", allcontaminants_results)

```

# Cox Proportional Hazards Model

```{r}
# Ensure metadata variables exist before mutation
salter_nocontaminants.rel.2 <- salter_nocontaminants.rel.2 %>%
  mutate(overall_progression_free = hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_nocontaminants$overall_progression_free,
         progressed = hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_nocontaminants$progressed)

salter_consensus.rel.2 <- salter_consensus.rel.2 %>%
  mutate(overall_progression_free = hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_consensus$overall_progression_free,
         progressed = hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_consensus$progressed)

salter_allcontaminants.rel.2 <- salter_allcontaminants.rel.2 %>%
  mutate(overall_progression_free = hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_allcontaminants$overall_progression_free,
         progressed = hila_samples_metadata_nodeath_allcontaminants$progressed)

# Correctly assigning numeric encoding
salter_nocontaminants.rel.2$progressed_numeric <- ifelse(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2$progressed == TRUE, 1, 0)
salter_consensus.rel.2$progressed_numeric <- ifelse(salter_consensus.rel.2$progressed == TRUE, 1, 0)
salter_allcontaminants.rel.2$progressed_numeric <- ifelse(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2$progressed == TRUE, 1, 0)

#UNIVARIATE ANALYSIS FOR NO CONTAMINANTS
str(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2)
univariate_results_nocontaminants <- lapply(names(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2)[1:(ncol(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2) - 3)], function(genus) {
  coxph(Surv(overall_progression_free, progressed_numeric) ~ salter_nocontaminants.rel.2[[genus]], data = salter_nocontaminants.rel.2)
})

# Extract results
univariate_summary_nocontaminants <- lapply(univariate_results_nocontaminants, summary)

univariate_df_nocontaminants <- data.frame(
  Genus = names(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2)[1:(ncol(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2) - 3)],
  HR = sapply(univariate_results_nocontaminants, function(model) exp(coef(model))),
  p_value = sapply(univariate_results_nocontaminants, function(model) summary(model)$coefficients[,"Pr(>|z|)"])
)

# View sorted results by p-value
univariate_df_nocontaminants[order(univariate_df_nocontaminants$p_value), ]
write.csv(file = "Univariate_DF_Nocontaminants.csv", univariate_df_nocontaminants)

#UNIVARIATE ANALYSIS FOR CONSENSUS
str(salter_consensus.rel.2)
univariate_results_consensus <- lapply(names(salter_consensus.rel.2)[1:(ncol(salter_consensus.rel.2) - 3)], function(genus) {
  coxph(Surv(overall_progression_free, progressed_numeric) ~ salter_consensus.rel.2[[genus]], data = salter_consensus.rel.2)
})

# Extract results
univariate_summary_consensus <- lapply(univariate_results_consensus, summary)

univariate_df_consensus <- data.frame(
  Genus = names(salter_consensus.rel.2)[1:(ncol(salter_consensus.rel.2) - 3)],
  HR = sapply(univariate_results_consensus, function(model) exp(coef(model))),
  p_value = sapply(univariate_results_consensus, function(model) summary(model)$coefficients[,"Pr(>|z|)"])
)

# View sorted results by p-value
univariate_df_consensus[order(univariate_df_consensus$p_value), ]
write.csv(file = "Univariate_DF_Consensus.csv", univariate_df_consensus)

#UNIVARIATE ANALYSIS FOR ALL CONTAMINANTS
str(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2)
univariate_results_allcontaminants <- lapply(names(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2)[1:(ncol(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2) - 3)], function(genus) {
  coxph(Surv(overall_progression_free, progressed_numeric) ~ salter_allcontaminants.rel.2[[genus]], data = salter_allcontaminants.rel.2)
})

# Extract results
univariate_summary_allcontaminants <- lapply(univariate_results_allcontaminants, summary)

univariate_df_allcontaminants <- data.frame(
  Genus = names(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2)[1:(ncol(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2) - 3)],
  HR = sapply(univariate_results_allcontaminants, function(model) exp(coef(model))),
  p_value = sapply(univariate_results_allcontaminants, function(model) summary(model)$coefficients[,"Pr(>|z|)"])
)

# View sorted results by p-value
univariate_df_allcontaminants[order(univariate_df_allcontaminants$p_value), ]
write.csv(file = "Univariate_DF_AllContaminants.csv", univariate_df_allcontaminants)

#MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS - NO CONTAMINANTS

multivariate_model_nocontaminants <- coxph(Surv(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2$overall_progression_free, 
                                                salter_nocontaminants.rel.2$progressed_numeric) ~ ., 
                                           data = salter_nocontaminants.rel.2[, 1:(ncol(salter_nocontaminants.rel.2) - 3)])
 
summary(multivariate_model_nocontaminants)

summary_multivariate_nocontaminants <- summary(multivariate_model_nocontaminants)

multivariate_results_nocontaminants_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(summary_multivariate_nocontaminants$coefficients),
  HR = exp(summary_multivariate_nocontaminants$coefficients[, "coef"]), 
  HR_lower95 = exp(summary_multivariate_nocontaminants$conf.int[, "lower .95"]),
  HR_upper95 = exp(summary_multivariate_nocontaminants$conf.int[, "upper .95"]), 
  p_value = summary_multivariate_nocontaminants$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]  
)

multivariate_results_nocontaminants_df <- multivariate_results_nocontaminants_df[order(multivariate_results_nocontaminants_df$p_value), ]
print(multivariate_results_nocontaminants_df)
write.csv(file = "Multivariate_Results_Nocontaminants.csv", multivariate_results_nocontaminants_df)

#MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS - CONSENSUS

multivariate_model_consensus <- coxph(Surv(salter_consensus.rel.2$overall_progression_free, 
                                                salter_consensus.rel.2$progressed_numeric) ~ ., 
                                           data = salter_consensus.rel.2[, 1:(ncol(salter_consensus.rel.2) - 3)])
 
summary(multivariate_model_consensus)

summary_multivariate_consensus <- summary(multivariate_model_consensus)

multivariate_results_consensus_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(summary_multivariate_consensus$coefficients),
  HR = exp(summary_multivariate_consensus$coefficients[, "coef"]), 
  HR_lower95 = exp(summary_multivariate_consensus$conf.int[, "lower .95"]),
  HR_upper95 = exp(summary_multivariate_consensus$conf.int[, "upper .95"]), 
  p_value = summary_multivariate_consensus$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]  
)

multivariate_results_consensus_df <- multivariate_results_consensus_df[order(multivariate_results_consensus_df$p_value), ]
print(multivariate_results_consensus_df)
write.csv(file = "Multivariate_Results_Consensus.csv", multivariate_results_consensus_df)

#MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS - ALL CONTAMINANTS

multivariate_model_allcontaminants <- coxph(Surv(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2$overall_progression_free, 
                                                salter_allcontaminants.rel.2$progressed_numeric) ~ ., 
                                           data = salter_allcontaminants.rel.2[, 1:(ncol(salter_allcontaminants.rel.2) - 3)])
 
summary(multivariate_model_allcontaminants)

summary_multivariate_allcontaminants <- summary(multivariate_model_allcontaminants)

multivariate_results_allcontaminants_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(summary_multivariate_allcontaminants$coefficients),
  HR = exp(summary_multivariate_allcontaminants$coefficients[, "coef"]), 
  HR_lower95 = exp(summary_multivariate_allcontaminants$conf.int[, "lower .95"]),
  HR_upper95 = exp(summary_multivariate_allcontaminants$conf.int[, "upper .95"]), 
  p_value = summary_multivariate_allcontaminants$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]  
)

multivariate_results_allcontaminants_df <- multivariate_results_allcontaminants_df[order(multivariate_results_allcontaminants_df$p_value), ]
print(multivariate_results_allcontaminants_df)
write.csv(file = "Multivariate_Results_AllContaminants.csv", multivariate_results_allcontaminants_df)

```
